---
title: "LRPITT05: Modified-ITT analysis of standardised receptive vocabulary (ROWPVT) (R)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

**In plain terms.** Did the children offered the intervention understand more words on a standardised receptive-vocabulary test (ROWPVT) than those who were not — and how large, and how certain, is that difference? Randomisation identifies the effect of *being offered* the programme within the fitted observed-data analysis set. Because the repository omits three randomised children and the model requires its analytic variables to be observed, this is an available-case modified-ITT contrast whose wider causal reading assumes ignorable missingness, not a full ITT of all 57 children.

LRPITT05 is the available-case modified-ITT analysis for standardised receptive vocabulary (ROWPVT) (`rowpvt`). The estimand is the randomised offer effect of group assignment $G$ on the post-score $R_{\text{post}}$ in the model-specific fitted sample, using the RCT phase only (time 1 $\rightarrow$ time 2).
This model supersedes LRP53.

_Identification and sign convention._ Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so randomisation identifies the offer contrast $\tau$ within the fitted analysis set without adjustment. Generalising it to all 57 randomised children additionally requires ignorable selection and missingness. The own baseline $\gamma_{\text{own}}$
and linear age $\gamma_A$ are **precision terms only** — they sharpen $\tau$ but are
not what licenses the causal interpretation, and no cross-baseline conditioning is
used. Attendance / sessions are never conditioned on (a DAG collider).
**Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means
the intervention raises the outcome.

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(R_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i},
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $R_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 170$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.
HSGPs on age and own baseline are off by default (the 2026-04-18 sensitivity
verdict).

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_itt.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
